verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted serine protease canonically produced by prostate epithelial cells. Recently (2025), cell-surface expression was definitively demonstrated in prostate cancer cell lines and patient-derived tumors by FACS, immunofluorescence, and confocal microscopy with confirmed colocalization to EpCAM. Multiple cell-surface-directed therapeutic modalities (bispecific T-cell engagers, CAR-T, radioligand conjugates) show preclinical and early clinical activity, confirming accessibility from the outer leaflet. However, surface expression is restricted to prostate cancer cells and is not a feature of normal baseline prostate biology—this represents cancer-cell-restricted surface display. The protein reaches the outer leaflet in this oncogenic context via a mechanism not yet fully elucidated (not a classical TM domain, likely a stabilized secretory-pool retention or membrane association), making it contextual rather than constitutive.
